title: QA: lsp.rs spawns language server with full inherited env (credential leak)
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/tools/lsp.rs:154-160
## Violation
LSP servers spawn without env-scrubbing equivalent to bash's `apply_env_scrub`. Every credential in the parent env (`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `AWS_*`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`) flows into rust-analyzer, pylsp, typescript-language-server. These servers commonly emit telemetry and write logs to disk.

Quoted (lsp.rs:154-160):
```
    let mut child = Command::new(server_cmd)
        .args(server_args)
        .stdin(Stdio::piped())
        .stdout(Stdio::piped())
        .stderr(Stdio::null())
        .spawn()
        .map_err(|e| format!("Failed to start {server_cmd}: {e}"))?;
```

OWASP A04. Bash subsystem has scrub; LSP path treated differently with no justification.
## Mandated refactor
1. Lift `apply_env_scrub` into a shared `tools::sandbox` and call from both bash and lsp spawn sites.
2. Switch to allowlist.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
